title: eriogonum saxatile var. bloomeri
intro: Eriogonum saxatile var. bloomeri, also known as Bloomer's Buckwheat Sagebrush, is a variety of shrub-like perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). Traditionally, the plant's roots and stems have been used to treat various health issues, with medicinal properties attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum saxatile var. bloomeri include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
